Into the Storm (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $47,602,194Details
International Box Office $102,827,177
Worldwide Box Office $150,429,371
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,790,788
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,992,362
Total $9,783,150
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In the span of a single day, the town of Silverton is ravaged by an unprecedented onslaught of tornadoes. The entire town is at the mercy of the erratic and deadly cyclones, even as storm trackers predict the worst is yet to come. Most people seek shelter, while others run towards the vortex, testing how far a storm chaser will go for that once-in-a-lifetime shot.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,346,427 (36.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.74 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:31.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$50,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,434 opening theaters/3,434 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $69,684,486

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 8th, 2014 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
August 8th, 2014 (IMAX) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: September 5th, 2014 (Wide), released as Into The Storm (Australia)
Video Release: November 18th, 2014 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of intense destruction and peril, and language including some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2309, 2/12/2014)
Running Time: 89 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Found Footage, Visual Effects
Plot point: Disaster, Field Scientists, Extreme Weather
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Broken Road
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

In the span of a single day, the town of Silverton is ravaged by an unprecedented onslaught of tornadoes. The entire town is at the mercy of the erratic and deadly cyclones, even as storm trackers predict the worst is yet to come. Most people seek shelter, while others run towards the vortex, testing how far a storm chaser will go for that once-in-a-lifetime shot.

Opening Weekend:$17,346,427 (36.4% of total gross)

Theater counts:3,434 opening theaters/3,434 max. theaters, 4.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $69,684,486

International Releases: September 5th, 2014 (Wide), released as Into The Storm (Australia)
Video Release: November 18th, 2014 by Warner Home Video
